info.name: ShopEase - Section API
info.description:
Curated Section management API endpoints for ShopEase E-commerce application.

**Base URL:** `{{base_url}}/api/v1`

**Authentication:** All endpoints require Bearer token (Sanctum). Get the token from the Login endpoint — it is automatically saved to the `token` collection variable.

**Valid Sections:** `best_sellers`, `new_arrivals`

**Rules:**
- A product can only be assigned to **one** section at a time.
- Each section supports a **maximum of 8 products**.

**Collection Variables:**
- `base_url` — server base URL
- `token` — Sanctum bearer token (auto-set on Login)
- `section_name` — section key to target (`best_sellers` or `new_arrivals`)
- `section_product_id` — auto-set when a product is assigned to a section

request.description: Login to get the auth token. The token is automatically saved to the `token` collection variable.
Get all sections (`best_sellers` and `new_arrivals`) each with their assigned products ordered by `sort_order`. **Requires authentication.**
Get the `best_sellers` section with all its assigned products ordered by `sort_order`. **Requires authentication.**
Get the `new_arrivals` section with all its assigned products ordered by `sort_order`. **Requires authentication.**

Assign one or more products to the specified section in a single request. **Requires authentication.**

**Rules:**
- `product_id` is a required array of product IDs.
- Each product must exist and must **not** already be assigned to any section.
- Duplicate IDs within the same request are rejected.
- The total number of new products must not exceed the section's remaining capacity (max 8 per section).
- `sort_order` is an optional array aligned by index with `product_id`. If omitted or shorter than `product_id`, missing positions default to their array index (0, 1, 2…).

The last created section-product record ID is automatically saved to the `section_product_id` collection variable.

**Body fields:**
- `product_id` *(required)* — Array of product IDs to assign, e.g. `[2, 4, 5]`.
- `sort_order` *(optional)* — Array of sort positions aligned with `product_id`, e.g. `[0, 1, 2]`.

Update the `sort_order` of a product within the specified section. **Requires authentication.**

The record must belong to the given section — a product in `best_sellers` cannot be updated via the `new_arrivals` path.

**Body fields:**
- `sort_order` *(required)* — New sort position (integer, min: 0).

Remove a product from the specified section. **Requires authentication.**

This only removes the section assignment — the product itself is **not** deleted. After removal the product can be re-assigned to any section.
